campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they paid tribute to K9 Lucas, a police department dog who recently passed away. The council also moved forward on several items including updates to beekeeping rules, a study about homeless services in the West Valley region, and reviewed the city's finances for the past year.
keyTopics: K9 Lucas memorial and tribute
Updated beekeeping ordinance approved
West Valley homeless services study and regional task force
Transit-oriented development discussion (Senate Bill 79)
City's fiscal year 2025 financial report accepted

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a special meeting to review and approve the work plans for five different city commissions that handle everything from parks and libraries to traffic safety and historic preservation. Each commission presented their priorities and goals for the next fiscal year. The council also held a private meeting to discuss a legal case the city may need to file.
keyTopics: Parks and recreation priorities for FY 2026-27
Heritage preservation and public art initiatives
Library services planning
Traffic safety improvements and goals
Planning commission development priorities

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met on March 24, 2026, to handle routine business including approving meeting minutes and financial reports, plus adopting new council procedures and rules. The council also approved flying Pride and Juneteenth flags, discussed affordable housing unit transfers, heard from the regional transit authority (VTA), and reviewed plans for implementing the city's housing strategy.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion for 2026
New affordable housing (BMR) unit transfer rules
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag approvals
VTA transportation presentation
Housing Element implementation schedule

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the city's finances from January, and check progress on housing and general planning goals. They also approved a final map for the Solana Project development, finalized plans for community health and senior services, and discussed changes to how meeting minutes will be formatted going forward.
keyTopics: Solana Project (Tract 10657) - final development map approved
January 2026 financial and investment report review
Housing Element and General Plan annual progress update
2026 Community Health and Senior Services work plan
New meeting minutes format and procedures

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to organize and plan upcoming council sessions. The council reviewed what items would be discussed at their April 7th meeting, decided which topics needed specific time slots, and checked whether any agenda items required special notice procedures. They also previewed the April 14th meeting agenda and reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: April 7, 2026 council meeting agenda finalization
April 14, 2026 draft agenda planning
Scheduling specific time slots for agenda items
Public records from March 19-26, 2026
Notice and waiver requirements for agenda items

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on March 30, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council also reviewed committee reports and discussed schedules for various boards and commissions throughout the city.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Board and commission meeting schedules
Document availability at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund local housing and community development programs. The commission approved meeting notes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking these funds to support housing and services in Sunnyvale.
keyTopics: CDBG federal grant funding applications and awards
Housing and community development projects seeking money
Approval of previous meeting minutes from February

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and thrive in Cupertino. They discussed ways to reach out to business owners about licensing requirements, partnering with shopping centers, running a summer campaign to encourage residents to shop locally, and offering financial incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to the city.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get their licenses in order
Outreach to shopping centers and malls in Cupertino
Summer of Celebrations shop local marketing campaign
Public-private partnerships to sponsor city events
Tax breaks and incentives to attract retail and startup companies

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026, where they discussed properties and sites of historical importance to the city. Residents could attend in person or join online via Zoom to participate in the discussion and share their opinions.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom options)
Public comment deadline at 12 p.m. on meeting day
Protection and review of historically significant properties

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting on March 17, 2026, to handle administrative housekeeping items like approving previous meeting notes and reviewing the schedule for upcoming meetings. The council also approved Mayor Montano's travel to an annual conference and confirmed a school district representative appointment to an economic committee.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and confirmed upcoming council meeting calendar
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual conference
School district representative appointed to Economic Development committee
City Manager given authority to negotiate and execute agreements (details incomplete)

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to recognize Women's History Month and approved two main agreements: one allowing the American Red Cross to use city facilities during disasters, and another to purchase email security software (Mimecast) for three years at about $93,400 annually.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software purchase ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda item added to meeting packet
